subject: [PATCH v3 1/4] memcg: store node_id instead of pglist_data pointer

2026-05-26.patch_summary: This patch modifies the struct obj_stock_pcp to store a node_id instead of a pointer to pglist_data, reducing memory usage on 64-bit machines by eliminating an 8-byte pointer. The change is made possible because NODE_DATA() can recover the pglist_data from the node id. A BUILD_BUG_ON() ensures that int16_t remains sufficient for storing node ids even if MAX_NUMNODES exceeds its maximum value in the future. This patch is part of a series and fixes an issue related to memcg conversion.
